tokens: Let me know when you're ready to deploy.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Babe, I am ready for you.
Everything I can see looks pretty good.
The walking hinges, you will be glad to know, are intact!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You want me to go up there and do that, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You ready for me to deploy?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: MESA insulation is not coming off as easy as in training.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: God, that LM is a pretty sight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let's see how good I am (at getting up the ladder).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get that thing again.
(Jack laughs as he jumps to the first rung) Yes, sir.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You bet your (pause, searching for a polite phrase) life I am.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Laughing) All I asked you to do was pull that handle up there.
(Pause) Man, anything you grab, Jack...I just grabbed this lanyard that was in the dust (and it) is really black.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You've got parallel chassis; the wheels look good on this side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get down and let's get it out.
(Pause) I'll wait for you to get the deploy cable.
I'll tell you, Jack, this place is not locally level.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There's not many places you could put the LM down and have it be zero, zero, zero (no pitch, no roll, no yaw).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know how much help I'm going to be.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How's your wheels on that side?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: They looked good a minute ago.
I got the Sun, so I can't tell much...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The only way to do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I may pull a Jim Irwin here.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Whoa, whoa, wait, wait, wait, wait.
Tried to get off the hinge there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I wish you could see it.
Jack, those wheels did not lock all the way up though.
We ought to pull them up before we...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, there it goes, by itself.
Let me pull it until the outriggers cables get slack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Walk away from it; that's easier (than pulling the tape hand over hand).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That tape up there on that reel...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me...Let me...Don't pull it until I...Okay.
(Pause) Man, I'd fall into that crater (meaning Poppie) if I went to the end of this line.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Houston, I do think we've got a different...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, we're deploying it at an angle.
The outrigger cables are free, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To Bob) Got a different breed of rock up here.
The stuff's sticking through this thin regolith...Or regolith, period.
I don't know whether it's thin or thick yet.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me get all this cable out of the way.
Otherwise I'll...Got enough of this stuff.
I don't like all that over there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: A geologist's paradise, if I ever saw one.
Boy, you certainly are changing the color of that cable, sir.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Just tried a John Young trick.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: But, I'm still getting my balance.
Just got to get some of this cable out of here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not sure my pockets are going to be accessible.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I'll tell you, I don't know how long this line to pull the Rover out is, but...(Pause) Well, I'll tell you, it sure is easy to get dusty, but that's nothing new to anybody.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think it's safe to say this surface was not formed yesterday.
There is a regolith; it looks classic.
Seriate distribution of particles up to 3 or 4 centimeters, anyway.
Then, you start to get maybe a selective distribution of large fragments.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to walk away with this one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Backing away from the LM) Here's a couple of different-looking rocks.
One's very white; one's quite dark.
But we do have a general rock type, I think, in the area.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jesus, how much cable is there?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There's a lot of it, Jack.
(Jack laughs) You're going to be a long way away.
We've got the front wheels on the surface, but keep going, I don't think you've got it up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I never thought I'd do geology this way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get up there and take a look.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Wait till you get to the ALSEP package.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: By golly, those wheels did lock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing in response to Bob's ALSEP remark) I never knew that cable was that long, Bob.
Oh, a glass-bottom crater with a little bench!
Looks like one of the Flagstaff explosion craters except for the glass in it.
That's the one I was talking about having a bright halo.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I don't know whether it's easier to walk out there or to do what I did in training.
(That I) said I wouldn't do on the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Referring to the cluster of deployment cables and tapes) Somebody's going to get tangled up with this thing.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's why I'd get it all under the LM somewhere.
It took me 5 minutes to do and get it all out of the way.
Okay, Bob, the front wheels locked in.
I had to pull the rear wheels back to get them to lock in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: At least no one let any air out of the tires.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, I look like I've been on the surface for a week already.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Think we can avoid that cable?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Why don't you set it there, pull this pin, and then you can go back and get it; that is, it's better to use the Rover contingency tool, because...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but that's off over there on the ground now, somewhere or another.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See if you can get that saddle loose.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, we're going to have to move that line, Jack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Face a little more east, so I don't have to run into the rim (of a crater on the initial test drive).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, Jack, you got some fenders and stuff for me...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Only semi-seriously) I was going to get my cable (out of the way under the LM).
I thought you said I could work on the cable.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You're putting me farther and farther behind.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can see a little yellow (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yours is in, but mine is not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: My outboard is in, but my inboard is not.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get the contingency tool and try to push those things closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here's a piece of glass I picked up.
I'm going to set it right on the floor of the Rover.
Jack, let me get that (contingency) tool.
We got to get those pins in, I think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you got any words on the yellow pins on the rear chassis?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The best way to put those in, if you've tried bouncing the chassis, would be to push them with the contingency tool.
Which I think is what you're going to do.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Long Pause) Well, I found how to get up!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Breathing heavily) Well, this thing (the contingency tool) was in the mud down here.
We'll find out in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me try to push it in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See if I can get mine in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you reach it from there?
Just a nice firm (push)...I'll hold it (the tool) on it (the pin).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Why don't you put that (tool) between the seats?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now where was I (in the checklist)?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to take it a little slower here in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just a little bit slower in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The blush is off the rose.
Okay, your front pin is in.
And both of mine are in.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Not quite as easy as in the training building.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, it's a case of knowing how to play in one-sixth g, is what it amounts to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get the seat down.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and I got the handle.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; make sure your T-(handle) locks.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm not...You're not all the way down yet, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I put a little piece of glass I picked up right by the Rover, here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to leave it right behind your footstool.
I had to pick it up.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, that's yours: your sample for the day.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I tell you, zero g is a piece of cake if you - (correcting himself) or one-sixth g - if you'd play it right.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, Gene, you've got fenders; (verifying the front hinge pins) your pin was good.
Mine are okay, and you'll have to check your outside ones.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: My two pins are good here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to pull your flags.
And your attitude indicator is free.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, look at that stuff go, will you?
Went over that...(Stops to listen to Bob)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Looks like your water temperature's getting pretty high.
You might want to go to intermediate cooling or slow down or something.
Looks like you're getting a little warm.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay, Jack, get that (Rover deployment) cable, because I tripped over it coming back.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and 17, you're right on schedule.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) Did you tell Captain America (meaning Ron Evans) we're on the surface?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We broke the news to him a while ago.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; the next spacecraft (that is, the LRV) to power-up is going to commence right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That takes care of that little job.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: How's my cooling look now, Bob?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's come down quite a bit.
You were eighty-six (degrees F), and now it's down to seven five.
We didn't want you to sweat.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I'm just a hot geologist; that's all.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Somebody (that is, Jack himself) kicked dirt all over the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if there is any life in this here baby.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Give me a yell when you start to go, and I'll try to be sure to be there with the camera.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, the seat belt fits perfect.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I thought I was going to get to drive.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Man, I got so much dust over my visor already, I got to wipe it off.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I've got to dust my visor off with something.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Don't use your glove or the
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Okay; when I was bringing that...(Responding to Bob) No, we'll use the
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's try to see if I can read in this Sun, now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let's wipe it out a couple of times to make sure we got all the steering.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: She goes forward and she goes reverse.
She's back in forward; and she's wiped out, and she's in park.
It ought to be on this one.
There's life in this here baby.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't know who's responsible for packing this ETB, but I think it was me.
You didn't by any chance pick up those
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're going to be hard to find, but I think we can do it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, they were right down there (on the ground below the porch), unless you picked them up.
That's exactly where the Rover tool was, too, and I picked it up, so they're probably there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: All the breakers closed except Nav.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Batteries are (pause to read the temperature gauges) 95 and 110.
Forward Motors are off-scale low, off-scale low; and Rears are off-scale low, off-scale low.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) (Talking to himself as he reads the decal and sets the circuit breakers) Drive Enable, Forward is going Prime 1.
And that is Both; I know that.
Okay; Steering, go Forward to A...Boy, it's hard to see in that Sun...and Rear to D.
Drive Power Forward going to A.
Now, I didn't feel any Earth-shaking rumbles like I do in the trainer, but let's see what happens.
I'm going to find out in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting tongue-tied) The runt...the fright...the front wheels turn.
I can't see the rear ones.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Finishing the pan) I'll verify them in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I can't see the rear ones, but I know the front ones turn.
Challenger's baby is on the roll.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And judging from the way it's handling, I think the rear wheels are steering too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, if they don't like this...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: As far as I can tell, you guys are right on within a minute or two.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: The Earth's just a little high for me, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm not sure I can get it without getting way away.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Somebody said it (Earth) was going to be just behind the South Massif.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to take a little spin around here, and I'll meet you at the front end (of the LM next to the MESA).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Boy, there's a lot of static, though, every time I start driving.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I know what that was over there, I think.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The basic material around the LM is just what I said: a fine-grained, medium-gray regolith-appearing material that has the standard seriate population.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The craters, though, (that are) bigger than about a meter in diameter seem to get to (that is, excavate) rock fragments (pause) which I haven't yet learned how to pick up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to give them our position here.
I think I know exactly where we are now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (To himself) Well, once you get them dirty, just like the boys say, it's hard to tell what they are.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm parked right next to Barjea.
And we (that is, the LM) are, from Barjea, (at) 12 o'clock...Jack, how far...Oh, you can't see.
I guess about 150 meters due west of Barjea.
And that's why we looked so close to Trident.
I'm coming right up on Poppie.
No question about where I am now.
I've got Trident, and when I get up there...We are abeam of Trident 1, just where I said we were.
We're about, oh, 100 meters just about due west of Poppie, which is almost in line with Barjea, of course; but basically (the LM is) on the (north-south) line, I think, between Rudolph and Trident 1.
And, as I look at it in cross section, about 100 meters north of Trident 1.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Jack, that is Trident right here that we walked over to.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Laughing, having fallen while trying to pick up a rock) I just got my first initiation to getting very dirty.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm coming right around the front now.
Houston, did you get that position?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And Bob, I'm...I'm...I'm very firm of that now.
I'm almost positive, unless I'm awfully mistaken about Trident.
I don't see how I could be from here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: At the sacrifice of my cleanliness, Houston, the basic light-colored rock type in the area looks very much like a cristobolite gabbros of the...I didn't see cristobolite, but it looks like the gabbros in the mare basalt suite.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Jack, I'm going to park...How about alongside (the MESA)?...Am I gonna screw up that little crater with glass in it if I park there?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm sure we'll find some more.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) (Looking at the dirt on Jack's suit) Where you been?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here, let me get the 15-volt supply.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think this camera is probably a little dirty on the lens.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: When you uncover one of those
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I want to put this camera over here right now, because it's pretty dirty to put back in that bag.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt
